title: Operator TUI: Optional Carbonyl browser render mode for CenterViewport
summary: Add an explicit, user-triggered browser render mode for the Operator TUI center viewport. The implementation is mostly present, but final end-to-end verification and task evidence still need cleanup before the full TODO can be marked done.

progress_assessment.reason: Core Carbonyl browser-mode files appear implemented, but TODO task evidence and E2E verification are not fully normalized in this JSON yet.

related_architecture_decisions.summary: Carbonyl center-browser rendering stays independent from both the legacy HTTP Hub relay transport and the new realtime WebRTC stack. This TODO only creates the explicit browser render surface. WebRTC/DataChannel behavior belongs to the separate realtime TODO.

task_state_contract.rule: Every implementation task has its own status. When a task is implemented and verified, change that task status to done and add done_at plus evidence. Do not mark the whole TODO done until all implementation_tasks are done.

existing_architecture_notes: Carbonyl is an explicit center render mode, not a replacement for ANSI/Sixel/Kitty rendering.
This TODO does not implement WebRTC signaling, RTCDataChannel, OIDC login, or Hub relay behavior. It only provides the explicit browser render surface used by those higher-level modes.

non_goals: Do not make Carbonyl the default renderer.
Do not route arbitrary web URLs through Carbonyl unless explicitly requested by the user.
Do not allow the Carbonyl subprocess to write outside the CenterViewport region.
Do not remove the existing Markdown/Mermaid ANSI fallback path.
Do not require Carbonyl for normal Operator TUI startup.
Do not implement WebRTC/DataChannel or OIDC token ownership in this TODO.

evidence: Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims capability_detector.py and view_capability_report.py changes.
Commit message reports carbonyl_browser capability flag and no regressions.
implementation_tasks.title: Detect Carbonyl availability as a degraded optional capability
implementation_tasks.remaining_follow_up: Keep this done unless test evidence later contradicts the commit claim.

evidence: Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims visual/browser/center_content_snapshot.py with CenterContentSnapshot and BrowserSnapshotMixin.
implementation_tasks.title: Add a CenterViewport content snapshot contract

evidence: client_surfaces/operator_tui/visual/browser/browser_document_adapter.py exists and is used by BrowserModeController.
Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims safe local HTML conversion and template.
implementation_tasks.title: Transform current center content into safe local HTML

evidence: client_surfaces/operator_tui/visual/browser/browser_mode_controller.py calls CarbonylRunner.start(), tick(), read_output(), write_input(), resize(), stop().
Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims PTY runner with resize and cleanup.
implementation_tasks.title: Run Carbonyl in a bounded PTY attached to CenterViewport

evidence: Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims commands.py adds center.browser.toggle/open_current/exit.
BrowserModeController exists with enter_browser_mode(), exit_browser_mode(), tick(), handle_input(), resize().
remaining_work: Verify actual wiring in keybindings_config.py, view_controller.py and CenterViewport runtime path.
Verify F7/Esc behavior in a manual or automated TUI run.
implementation_tasks.title: Wire browser mode into Operator TUI commands and footer hints

evidence: Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims URL/temp-file policy and remote disabled by default.
implementation_tasks.title: Add safe defaults for local/remote browser rendering

evidence: Commit d24c1ec57ceaff81bc200e6b5183c59f93176e18 claims 61 new tests and scripts/operator_tui_carbonyl_smoke.py.
.venv/bin/python scripts/operator_tui_carbonyl_smoke.py => adapter pipeline OK; Carbonyl binary absent on PATH, degraded mode reported correctly.
implementation_tasks.title: Add tests and a smoke command for browser mode

mermaid_architecture:
flowchart TD
    A[Active CenterViewport content] --> B[CenterContentSnapshot]
    B --> C[BrowserDocumentAdapter]
    C --> D[Safe local HTML file/data URL]
    D --> E[CarbonylRunner PTY subprocess]
    E --> F[BrowserModeController]
    F --> G[Clipped terminal frames inside CenterViewport]
    H[Keyboard/mouse input] -->|only while browser mode active| F
    I[Exit browser mode] --> J[Stop Carbonyl and restore previous VisualView]
    K[OIDC/WebRTC modes] -. use browser surface only .-> F
acceptance_summary: Explicit TUI command/keybinding wiring still needs verification before full TODO closure.
Test/smoke evidence must be recorded from a real run before marking the whole TODO done.
Carbonyl mode remains explicit-only and never the default.
